understanding.goal_understanding: Navigate the level by moving the currently selected piece (and possibly using other selectable pieces) to reach the goal tile(s) within a limited step budget, potentially requiring repositioning/pushing blocks to clear paths.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempts to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction (e.g., [21,21] -> [18,21] is one cell left). If a pushable block is immediately adjacent in the movement direction and the space beyond it is free, it can be pushed along with the selected piece; otherwise movement fails. Walls/boundaries can block movement, and different wall-like edges may behave differently, so movement attempts are sometimes needed to discover their true behavior. Levels may contain multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK on an on-screen selectable's exact pixel/grid position switches control to that piece. The movement_blocked flags are only hints that something is adjacent and do not guarantee success/failure.
